items.text: Our principal U.S. production and R&D operations, in alphabetical order, are located in California, Connecticut, Delaware, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, and Texas.
items.rationale: Simple factual listing of manufacturing/R&D locations, no risk content.

items.text: We believe our diverse manufacturing base sets us apart, especially at a time when supply chain resiliency is strongly valued by our customers.
items.rationale: Positive marketing statement about manufacturing diversity, not a risk disclosure.

items.text: Occupational Health and Safety .
items.rationale: Section header with no substantive content.

items.text: Talent Acquisition, Employee Development, and Learning .
items.rationale: Section header with no substantive content.

items.text: In connection with universities, we are focused on an internship and apprentice program that builds our early career hire talent pool.
items.rationale: Describes an internship/apprentice program; informational, not a risk.

items.text: Overall, our key differentiators are our deep technology expertise combined with our ability to deliver volume solutions at scale.
items.rationale: Generic statement about competitive differentiators, not a risk.

items.text: We use rare-earth materials in some of our production processes.
items.rationale: Introduces a new specific dependency on rare-earth materials, which implies supply chain/material risk not previously disclosed.

items.text: Like with other materials, we are constantly working to strengthen and diversify our supply chain for resilience.
items.rationale: Generic mitigation language about supply chain resilience efforts, no new risk detail.

items.text: These strategies include maintaining additional buffer stocks and securing multiple sources to ensure resilience.
items.rationale: Describes mitigation strategies (buffer stock, multiple sourcing), not a new risk.

items.text: Networking • Transceivers, systems, subsystems, modules, components, optics, and semiconductor devices for datacenter and communications applications.
items.rationale: Product listing for Networking segment, descriptive only.

items.text: Materials 8 • Engineered materials, laser optics, thermoelectric components, and advanced ceramic and metal-matrix composite materials and products. • VCSELs, EELs, pump lasers, high-power lasers for materials processing, and ICs.
items.rationale: Product listing for Materials segment, descriptive only.

items.text: Lasers • Excimer lasers, solid-state lasers, CO 2 lasers, and laser systems for a variety of industrial applications, including semiconductor capital equipment, display manufacturing, precision manufacturing, and scientific research. • Laser systems and subsystems.
items.rationale: Product listing for Lasers segment, descriptive only.

items.text: Markets For fiscal year 2025, we report revenues of our business segments in the following markets: communications, industrial, instrumentation and electronics.
items.rationale: Describes revenue reporting categories, administrative detail.

items.text: Effective July 1, 2025, for our fiscal year 2026, the Company realigned its organizational structure into two reporting segments which mirror the markets that they report into: (i) Datacenter and Communications, and (ii) Industrial.
items.rationale: Describes organizational segment realignment, structural/administrative, not a risk statement.

items.text: We have been experiencing significant growth in AI and ML in the datacom market vertical.
items.rationale: Notes growth in AI/ML datacom market, a positive business trend not framed as risk.

items.text: We have in-house laser design and manufacturing capability for GaAs-based VCSELs, InP-based DMLs, EMLs, and CW lasers.
items.rationale: Describes in-house manufacturing capability, descriptive only.

items.text: Our portfolio also includes silicon photonics and a broad array of CPO-enabling technologies. • Telecom Market Vertical.
items.rationale: Describes product portfolio additions, descriptive only.

items.text: Our Telecom products include coherent transmission components and transceivers as well as transport products such as 980 nm and 14xx pump lasers, erbium-doped fiber amplifiers, passive components, OCM, OTDR, ROADM and Optical line systems.
items.rationale: Lists telecom product offerings, descriptive only.

items.text: Our excimer laser-based annealing systems can improve precision, combining high-spatial precision and selectivity for LTPS OLED display production.
items.rationale: Describes technical capabilities of laser systems, descriptive only.

items.text: Our CO 2 lasers and UV ultrashort-pulsed lasers are used for cutting applications.
items.rationale: Describes laser applications, descriptive only.

items.text: 11 During the fiscal year ended June 30, 2025, we focused our R&D investments in the following areas: Networking Area of Development: Our R&D Investments: Photonics design Continue to develop and improve crystal materials, precision optical parts, and laser device components for photonics applications.
items.rationale: Describes R&D investment areas, descriptive only.

items.text: Datacom transceivers and components for datacom transceivers 800G/1.6T transceivers, CPO, VCSELs, EMLs, silicon photonics, ICs, isolators, and thermoelectric coolers; 400G/lane components supporting 3.2T and 6.4T.
items.rationale: Lists datacom transceiver technologies, descriptive only.

items.text: Optical circuit switch (OCS) Develop the OCS product family for AI/ML and hyperscale datacenters based on our digital liquid-crystal technology.
items.rationale: Describes OCS product development plans, descriptive only.

items.text: Coherent transceivers Drive further integration to reduce size and power consumption; increase bandwidth to enable 100G/200G/400G/800G coherent transceivers.
items.rationale: Describes coherent transceiver R&D goals, descriptive only.

items.text: Pump lasers Continue to invest in our next-generation GaAs pump laser portfolio to address evolving terrestrial and undersea markets.
items.rationale: Describes pump laser R&D plans, descriptive only.

items.text: Advanced optics manufacturing High-precision and more compact optics and automated assembly platforms and packages.
items.rationale: Describes optics manufacturing R&D focus, descriptive only.

items.text: Materials Area of Development: Our R&D Investments: High-power lasers and semiconductor lasers Increase output power and reliability of EEL diodes, VCSELs, InP lasers, and detectors for a variety of applications across our markets.
items.rationale: Describes laser/semiconductor R&D goals, descriptive only.

items.text: SiC epitaxial wafers Continuous improvement to maintain world-class, high-quality, large-diameter substrates and epitaxial wafers; develop SiC epitaxial wafers.
items.rationale: Describes SiC wafer R&D goals, descriptive only.

items.text: Thermoelectric materials and devices Continue to develop leading Bi 2 Te 3 materials for thermoelectric cooling/heating; focus on thermoelectric power-generation capability in order to introduce new products.
items.rationale: Describes thermoelectric material R&D goals, descriptive only.

items.text: Lasers Area of Development: Our R&D Investments: Diode-pumped solid-state lasers Continue to develop solid-state lasers for industrial applications for materials processing, instrumentation, and scientific applications.
items.rationale: Describes solid-state laser R&D goals, descriptive only.

items.text: Ultrafast fiber lasers Continue to develop industrial femtosecond fiber lasers for semiconductor capital equipment and display manufacturing.
items.rationale: Describes fiber laser R&D goals, descriptive only.

items.text: Femtosecond oscillators and amplifiers Continue to develop ultrafast laser systems for scientific applications.
items.rationale: Describes ultrafast laser R&D goals, descriptive only.

items.text: Optically pumped semiconductor lasers Continue to broaden the product portfolio of CW, visible, and ultraviolet OPSLs.
items.rationale: Describes OPSL product development, descriptive only.

items.text: Semiconductor lasers Increase output power of GaAs- and InP-based edge-emitting semiconductor lasers for laser pumping, and industrial applications.
items.rationale: Describes semiconductor laser R&D goals, descriptive only.

items.text: Excimer lasers and excimer laser tools Continue to support existing excimer laser-based applications in display manufacturing, instrumentation, and materials processing.
items.rationale: Describes excimer laser applications, descriptive only.

items.text: 12 CO 2 lasers Continue to develop CO 2 lasers used in industrial applications.
items.rationale: Describes CO2 laser R&D goals, descriptive only.

items.text: Laser tools Continue to develop laser-based tools used for marking, cutting, and welding applications.
items.rationale: Describes laser tool R&D goals, descriptive only.

items.text: R&D expenditures were $582 million, $479 million, and $500 million for the fiscal years 2025, 2024, and 2023, respectively.
items.rationale: Provides R&D expenditure figures, factual financial data, not a risk statement.

items.text: He brings over 25 years of experience in the technology and semiconductor industries, with a strong track record in innovation-driven businesses.
items.rationale: Executive biography detail, not related to risk factors.

items.text: He also sits on the Americas Executive Board for the MIT Sloan School of Management and the U.S.-Japan Business Council.
items.rationale: Executive biography detail, not related to risk factors.
